pr.title: Rename code_8023B0F8 to mnnamenew, since it is known and split
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Renamed the menu assembly object/file from the placeholder address-based name code_8023B0F8 to the known module name mnnamenew and updated obj_files.mk so the build references the renamed object. The assembly file itself was a 100% similarity rename, so this PR was a naming/build-list cleanup rather than a code change.
what_changed: Renamed asm/melee/mn/code_8023B0F8.s to asm/melee/mn/mnnamenew.s with 100% similarity.
Updated TEXT_O_FILES in obj_files.mk from $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/mn/code_8023B0F8.o to $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/mn/mnnamenew.o.
Kept the file in the mn/menu assembly object sequence near mnname.o, mndiagram.o, mndiagram2.o, and mndiagram3.o.
smart_moves: Replaced an address-derived placeholder filename with a known module-style name once the file identity was known.
Updated the build object list at the same time as the file rename, avoiding a stale obj_files.mk reference.
Used a pure rename with no assembly content changes, keeping the PR low-risk and easy to verify.
decomp_lessons: When an unknown asm slice becomes identified, rename both the source assembly file and its corresponding object entry in obj_files.mk.
For decomp organization, prefer meaningful module names such as mnnamenew over address placeholders like code_8023B0F8 once the name is known.
Small naming-only PRs can help keep the repository searchable and reduce ambiguity before or during later C splits.
naming_conventions: Address-placeholder asm names used the pattern code_XXXXXXXX, exemplified by code_8023B0F8.
Menu-related files under asm/melee/mn use mn-prefixed lowercase module names, exemplified by mnname, mnnamenew, mndiagram, mndiagram2, and mndiagram3.
The renamed module is spelled mnnamenew in both the asm path and the object-file path.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: This was a 100% similarity assembly file rename, not a matching-code change.
The build list entry was changed to the renamed .o path so the same assembly content continues to be linked under the new filename.
No evidence of function-level matching tactics, compiler behavior, or symbol changes was present in the PR slice.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that decompile or split asm/melee/mn/mnnamenew.s.
Search for remaining code_XXXXXXXX placeholders in asm/melee/mn that may have known module names.
Check whether mnnamenew receives headers, C source, or symbol names in subsequent menu decomp work.
